name: Zhang He (Junyi)
page_title: Zhang He (Junyi) 張郃 (儁乂) [Han Fu, Yuan Shao, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	After his defeat by Zhang Fei in the novel, Cao Hong’s reaction suggests that Zhang He was humiliated. Historically, because he captured two commanderies in the same campaign, Zhang He was promoted after his defeat at the hands of Zhang Fei. Cao Hong was not involved.
	In the novel, Sima Yi comes up with the plan to surround Ma Su. In history, it was Zhang He’s idea.
	In the novel, Zhang He suggests that he pursue Wei Yan, which Sima Yi argues against. Historically, Sima Yi suggested that Zhang He pursue Zhuge Liang’s army, and Zhang He argued against it. Wei Yan was not involved.

Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	After his defeat by Zhang Fei in the novel, Cao Hong’s reaction suggests that Zhang He was humiliated. Historically, because he captured two commanderies in the same campaign, Zhang He was promoted after his defeat at the hands of Zhang Fei. Cao Hong was not involved.
	In the novel, Sima Yi comes up with the plan to surround Ma Su. In history, it was Zhang He’s idea.
	In the novel, Zhang He suggests that he pursue Wei Yan, which Sima Yi argues against. Historically, Sima Yi suggested that Zhang He pursue Zhuge Liang’s army, and Zhang He argued against it. Wei Yan was not involved.

full_text:
Zhang He (Junyi) 張郃 (儁乂)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhang He (張郃), styledJunyi (儁乂), was born in Mao county, Hejian commandery (Presently Ren Qiu in He Bei Province). Originally under Yuan Shao, defected to Wei. Died battling Zhuge Liang. Zhang He was affiliated with the Han Fu Dynasty, Yuan Shao, and the Wei Kingdom. Zhang He lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
After his defeat by Zhang Fei in the novel, Cao Hong’s reaction suggests that Zhang He was humiliated. Historically, because he captured two commanderies in the same campaign, Zhang He was promoted after his defeat at the hands of Zhang Fei. Cao Hong was not involved.
In the novel, Sima Yi comes up with the plan to surround Ma Su. In history, it was Zhang He’s idea.
In the novel, Zhang He suggests that he pursue Wei Yan, which Sima Yi argues against. Historically, Sima Yi suggested that Zhang He pursue Zhuge Liang’s army, and Zhang He argued against it. Wei Yan was not involved.
